note: Proven-solution knowledge base for the THINK mechanism (bin/lib/think.ts). Append-only: every entry MUST cite ≥1 authoritative source (no source → not proven → not here). The mechanism LEARNS by appending newly researched+verified fixes. Order matters: classify() returns the first pattern that matches.

entries.provenSolution: Replace lock-retry mutex with a FIFO ticket-lock (Lamport bakery): monotonic next + serving counter, served strictly in arrival order → provably starvation-free. Dead-holder force-advance for liveness.
sources: Lamport bakery algorithm (textbook, provably starvation-free)
Ollama FAQ docs.ollama.com/faq (NUM_PARALLEL=1 + MAX_QUEUE processed in order)
Node worker-pool starvation (medium Nexumo/Duckweave): fair scheduling + per-class limits

entries.provenSolution: Exponential backoff with FULL JITTER: delay=random(0,1)*min(cap, base*2^attempt), capped, bounded retries; classify transient vs non-transient and FAIL FAST on non-transient (circuit-breaker). Operations must be idempotent.
sources: AWS Prescriptive Guidance — Retry with backoff (docs.aws.amazon.com/prescriptive-guidance)
AWS Builders' Library — Timeouts, retries, and backoff with jitter

entries.provenSolution: Bound concurrency per class (do not over-subscribe): single-GPU = at most 1 local + N cloud; add skip-done idempotency so already-satisfied work is not re-run. Run heavy gates only when the machine is quiesced.
sources: Node worker-pool starvation/over-subscription (medium Nexumo/Duckweave): per-class limits
Ollama FAQ: OLLAMA_NUM_PARALLEL default 1 (memory-bound queue)

entries.provenSolution: Conductor escalation: when the fleet models cannot produce a gated result, the conductor reads the REAL source file and authors the proposal itself (evidence-based, honest attribution model:'claude-conductor'). Not a model faking — the conductor solving what it can. No half-work.
sources: operator directive 'sen çözebileceğin konularda yönlendir' + 'yarım-iş yok' (this project)
evidence-based: every proposal cites the real file it read

entries.provenSolution: One-shot wrappers exit → the terminal closes the tab. Run a PERSISTENT agent that idle-heartbeats and never exits, and append `exec $SHELL` so the tab survives even if the agent returns.
sources: macOS Terminal/iTerm2 'close when shell exits' default behavior (verified live: tab vanished after fast worker)

entries.provenSolution: osascript tab discovery blocks synchronously (~5s without Automation permission). Make it opt-in (ORCH_TABS=1) and bound every osascript call with a timeout; never on the hot path.
sources: RISK-ORCH-008 (this project, verified) + orchestration/bin/discover.ts timeout guard

entries.provenSolution: Extract the shared logic into ONE module and import it (Don't Repeat Yourself); if the two only look similar but differ in intent, record a justified suppression instead of merging.
sources: Martin Fowler, Refactoring (Extract Function/Module)
The Pragmatic Programmer — DRY principle

entries.provenSolution: Add a unit test for the pure core first (fast, deterministic); prefer testing IO-free functions. A pure module with property tests closes the gap without brittle IO mocking.
sources: Testing Pyramid (Mike Cohn / Martin Fowler): favor fast unit tests over IO-bound ones
this project: pure-core + thin-IO split (gpu-lock/backoff/think tested pure)

entries.provenSolution: Wire the artifact into a consumer (produced JSON must be consumed) OR delete it. An orphan that is generated-but-never-read is either half-work (wire it) or dead code (remove it).
sources: this project RISK-ORCH-015 (orphan-tool wiring: produce+consume together)
dead-code elimination (standard practice)
entries.evidence: autopilot chain consumes critic/dod/fuse/think outputs (no orphan generators)

entries.pattern: flaky|test timed out|Test timed out in|probe.*hang|timeout.*collision|timed out in \d+ms
entries.provenSolution: A test that spawns a subprocess doing a network/health probe MUST use a probe timeout FAR below the test's own timeout (fail-fast). Colliding equal timeouts (probe 5000ms == test 5000ms) race under load → flaky. Fix: probe timeout « test timeout (e.g. 1500ms probe vs 5000ms test).
sources: health-check/liveness fail-fast timeout best-practice (probe short, bounded)
this project: self_heal.mjs probe 5000→1500 → scripts/self-heal.test.ts stable, FULL-gate green with NO GATE_SKIP (vO28)

entries.provenSolution: Green work must be committed at the phase boundary (quality gate: tsc → tests → conventional commit). Uncommitted-green is half-work; commit when the FULL gate passes (quiesce heavy load first so the gate isn't flaky).
sources: AGENTS.md §3 quality gate (this project)
trunk-based development: small, gated, frequent commits
entries.evidence: each vO phase closed with a gated conventional commit (d1cce40 etc.)

entries.provenSolution: Establish bidirectional requirements traceability: every roadmap item traces FORWARD to an artifact (tool/test/commit) and every artifact traces BACKWARD to a requirement. Maintain a traceability matrix (roadmap ↔ bin/*.ts tool-map), and flag both orphan requirements (a roadmap line with no implementation) and orphan artifacts (a tool with no roadmap line). Keep roadmap and tool-map in sync so coverage stays auditable rather than drifting.
sources: Gotel & Finkelstein 1994 — 'An Analysis of the Requirements Traceability Problem', Proc. 1st IEEE Int'l Conf. on Requirements Engineering (ICRE), pp. 94-101
ISO/IEC/IEEE 29148:2018 — Systems and software engineering — Life cycle processes — Requirements engineering (bidirectional traceability requirement)
entries.evidence: orchestration/ROADMAP_ORCHESTRATION.md 'Araç–Versiyon İzlenebilirlik' tool-map (7-tool v1.28.1 subsection) + dod.ts roadmap-incoherence rule → DOD.json

entries.provenSolution: A tool is not 'Done' until its Definition-of-Done triple — {unit test, roadmap row, SEYIR (logbook) entry} — is satisfied concurrently at the phase boundary. On a concurrent-task lapse, complete the MISSING members together (write the pure-core test, add the roadmap row, record the SEYIR entry) instead of shipping partial work; batch newer tools through a one-shot governance-reconcile sweep. This is the union of the coverage-gap + roadmap-coherence + governance rules — the same 'no partial Done' discipline applied across all three artifacts at once.
sources: Schwaber & Sutherland — The Scrum Guide 2020, 'Definition of Done' (an Increment is unusable until it meets the shared DoD, which includes tests + documentation → no partial 'Done')
this project: sibling DOD rules already proven+registered (coverage-gap vO20, uncommitted-green vO-all, roadmap-coherence v1.28.1) — concurrent-task (R6) is their conjunction
entries.evidence: orchestration/bin/lib/dod.ts auditConcurrent (R6 rule) + vO43 governance reconcile (commit 1201852: 27 tools' roadmap-row + SEYIR-entry + (missing) test completed eş-zamanlı; tests/fleet-launch.test.ts + tests/oracle.test.ts added)
